action.hypothesis: After three non-improving rounds on pure K descent, the bucketed backbone likely needs recipe exploitation rather than further sparsity pressure. A stronger auxiliary feature-usage penalty may help the low-K regime use its limited capacity more efficiently, so testing `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625` at the best current small-K bucketed point (`K=16`) is the cleanest one-variable probe.
action.summary: Run a pure auxiliary-loss sweep on `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=16`, keeping architecture, optimizer, LR, preprocessing, and `EF=16` fixed while changing only `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.0` to `0.0625`.
action.self_review: This follows the explicit exploitation guidance, avoids new architectures, and changes exactly one parameter. I chose the `K=16` bucketed point instead of high-K anchors because it is the current practical lower bound for this family after `K=8` failed, so improving it is more valuable for the Pareto frontier than further polishing `K=128`.
notes_to_memory: Round 144 switches from K-axis exploration to exploitation after three non-improving rounds and tests whether stronger auxiliary usage pressure can improve the current low-K bucketed recipe.
This round isolates `AUXK_ALPHA` on `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=16`, which is the most valuable current small-K anchor after `K=8` regressed too far.
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625` improves `K=16`, propagate the same aux setting to `K=24` or `K=32` to check whether the gain is a real family-level recipe improvement.
If it underperforms but training looks smooth, try the exploitation LR sweep next at `K=16` with `LR=4e-4` before concluding the current bucketed recipe is saturated.
If it destabilizes or clearly worsens convergence, keep `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` as the bucketed baseline and move to an LR-only probe on the same `K=16` anchor.

result.description: Run a pure auxiliary-loss sweep on `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=16`, keeping architecture, optimizer, LR, preprocessing, and `EF=16` fixed while changing only `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.0` to `0.0625`.
result.self_review: This follows the explicit exploitation guidance, avoids new architectures, and changes exactly one parameter. I chose the `K=16` bucketed point instead of high-K anchors because it is the current practical lower bound for this family after `K=8` failed, so improving it is more valuable for the Pareto frontier than further polishing `K=128`.
